LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION, DAVID GOSSAGE, G. MICHAEL HARRINGTON, DAVID SANDLER, PATRICK PETERSON, AND, GERALD KELLY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 8TH (ASSAULT), 9TH (BATTERY), 11TH (NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS), AND 12TH (DEFAMATION) CAUSES OF ACTION. 8TH, 9TH, & 12TH ARE TIME-BARRED. 11TH CAUSE OF ACTION BARRED BY WORKER'S COMPENSATION EXCLUSIVITY PROVISION. SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 10TH CAUSE OF ACTION TO PLEAD COMPLIANCE WITH TORT CLAIMS ACT AND TO ALLEGE CONDUCT WITHIN THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-05-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION, DAVID GOSSAGE, G. MICHAEL HARRINGTON, DAVID SANDLER, PATRICK PETERSON, and, GERALD KELLY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 8th (assault), 9th (battery), 11th (negligent infliction of emotional distress), and 12th (Defamation) causes of action. 8th, 9th, & 12th are time-barred. 11th cause of action barred by worker's compensation exclusivity provision. Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to 10th cause of action to plead compliance with Tort Claims Act and to allege conduct within the statute of limitations. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
